# Qualification Discovery Questionnaire

> Structured conversation guide for the initial Seller qualification meeting. Organized by the Meritage 5P Framework. This is not a form to be handed to the prospect — it is a guide for the advisor conducting the conversation.
>
> **Template type:** Qualification tool
> **Used in:** Module 02 (Qualification)
> **Paired with:** client-qualification-scorecard.md
>
> ---
>
> ## How to Use This Guide
>
> This questionnaire structures the discovery conversation — typically 60–90 minutes — with a prospective Seller. It is designed to be conversational, not interrogative. The advisor should ask questions naturally, follow threads that surface in the conversation, and return to this guide to ensure no area is missed.
>
> Not every question will apply to every prospect. Use judgment about which questions are most relevant given what you already know.
>
> Take notes throughout. The notes from this conversation are the primary input to the 5P Assessment and Qualification Scorecard.
>
> ---
>
> ## Pre-Meeting Checklist
>
> Before the conversation, confirm the following:
> - [ ] Basic research on the business completed (industry, approximate size, years in operation)
> - [ ] - [ ] Referral context documented (who made the introduction, what they shared)
> - [ ] - [ ] Preliminary market intelligence gathered (recent transactions in this sector, active buyers)
> - [ ] - [ ] Any public information about the owner reviewed
> - [ ] - [ ] Questions prioritized based on pre-meeting research
>
> - [ ] ---
>
> - [ ] ## Opening (5 minutes)
>
> - [ ] *Set the tone: this is a mutual evaluation, not a pitch. Meritage is deciding whether to take the engagement as much as the prospect is deciding whether to engage Meritage.*
>
> - [ ] Suggested opening: *"I appreciate the time today. I'd like to spend this conversation learning about your business and understanding what you're thinking about a potential transaction. At the same time, I want to be honest that we're also evaluating fit — we're selective about the engagements we take, and I want to make sure this is the right relationship for both of us. Does that make sense?"*
>
> - [ ] ---
>
> - [ ] ## Section 1: People
>
> - [ ] *Lens: Who owns and runs this business, and what happens to each of them in a sale?*
>
> - [ ] ### Ownership and Motivation
>
> - [ ] - Tell me about yourself and how you got into this business.
> - [ ] - How long have you owned the business? Is it your family's business, or did you build it from scratch?
> - [ ] - What's prompting you to think about a sale now? What's the driver?
> - [ ] - On a scale of 1 to 10, how motivated are you to complete a transaction in the next 12–18 months? What would move that number?
> - [ ] - Have you been through a sale process before — either as a buyer or seller?
> - [ ] - Is there any timeline pressure I should know about? *(health, retirement, financial need, partnership situation)*
>
> - [ ] ### Owner Role and Dependence
>
> - [ ] - Walk me through a typical week for you. What do you personally spend your time on?
> - [ ] - If you took a 3-month leave of absence tomorrow, what would happen? Who would handle your responsibilities?
> - [ ] - Which customer relationships are primarily yours personally? How many years have those relationships been in place?
> - [ ] - What would you say is the one thing about this business that would be hardest to replace if you left?
>
> - [ ] ### Management Team
>
> - [ ] - Who else is on the leadership team? Tell me about them.
> - [ ] - How long have your key managers been with you?
> - [ ] - Are any key employees aware you're thinking about a sale? Have you had any retention conversations?
> - [ ] - After a close, what is your plan? Are you open to staying involved in a transition, and for how long?
>
> - [ ] ### Cultural Fit *(observe, don't ask directly)*
>
> - [ ] *Note the following during the conversation:*
> - [ ] - [ ] Does the owner listen and engage with questions, or do they redirect and minimize?
> - [ ] - [ ] Do they speak candidly about weaknesses, or do they deflect?
> - [ ] - [ ] Are they realistic about what the process involves, or do they expect it to be simple?
> - [ ] - [ ] Do they ask good questions about how Meritage works?
>
> - [ ] ---
>
> - [ ] ## Section 2: Performance
>
> - [ ] *Lens: What has the business actually done, and how defensible are those results?*
>
> - [ ] ### Revenue and Growth
>
> - [ ] - What is your approximate annual revenue for the last three years?
> - [ ] - Has revenue been growing, declining, or flat? What's driven the trend?
> - [ ] - Do you have any large contracts or long-term customer agreements? What percentage of revenue is under contract?
> - [ ] - Is the business seasonal? Are there revenue concentration periods I should know about?
>
> - [ ] ### EBITDA and Margins
>
> - [ ] - What is your approximate EBITDA for the last year? For the prior two years?
> - [ ] - What are the major addbacks you'd want included in a normalized EBITDA calculation? *(owner salary above market, personal expenses, one-time costs)*
> - [ ] - Are there any expenses in the business that a new owner wouldn't have — family members on payroll, personal vehicles, above-market real estate leases?
> - [ ] - Have you ever had a financial review or quality-of-earnings done?
>
> - [ ] ### Financial Statement Quality
>
> - [ ] - Who prepares your financial statements — a CPA firm, or internally?
> - [ ] - Are your financials accrual-basis or cash-basis?
> - [ ] - Do you have three full years of P&L statements and tax returns available?
> - [ ] - Are your business and personal finances fully separate, or is there any commingling?
>
> - [ ] ### Customer and Revenue Quality
>
> - [ ] - Who are your top 5 customers? What percentage of revenue does each represent?
> - [ ] - How long have your top customers been with you? Are those relationships contractual or relationship-based?
> - [ ] - Have you lost any significant customers in the last three years? What happened?
> - [ ] - What is your customer retention rate?
>
> - [ ] ---
>
> - [ ] ## Section 3: Position
>
> - [ ] *Lens: Where does this business stand in its market, and why does it win?*
>
> - [ ] ### Competitive Position
>
> - [ ] - In plain terms, what does the business do?
> - [ ] - Who are your primary competitors? How do you differ from them?
> - [ ] - Why do customers choose you over a competitor? What's the honest answer — not the marketing answer?
> - [ ] - Have you lost business to competitors recently? Why did you lose it?
>
> - [ ] ### Market Position
>
> - [ ] - How would you describe your position in the market — are you a leader, a niche player, a regional player?
> - [ ] - Are there licensing requirements, certifications, or regulatory approvals that create barriers to entry in your business?
> - [ ] - Do you have any proprietary technology, processes, or intellectual property?
> - [ ] - How would you describe the health of your industry right now? Growing, stable, declining?
>
> - [ ] ### Brand and Reputation
>
> - [ ] - What is your business's reputation in the market? How would your customers describe you?
> - [ ] - Have you won any industry recognition or awards?
> - [ ] - Are there any reputational issues or customer disputes I should know about?
>
> - [ ] ---
>
> - [ ] ## Section 4: Potential
>
> - [ ] *Lens: What can a buyer do with this business that you haven't?*
>
> - [ ] ### Growth Levers
>
> - [ ] - If you had unlimited capital and time, what would you do differently to grow this business?
> - [ ] - Are there geographic markets you haven't entered that represent a real opportunity?
> - [ ] - Are there adjacent products or services your customers have asked for that you haven't offered?
> - [ ] - Is there a technology or operational improvement that would meaningfully change your cost structure or capabilities?
>
> - [ ] ### Platform Potential
>
> - [ ] - Do you see your business as a potential platform for a roll-up in your industry?
> - [ ] - Are there smaller competitors you could acquire, or that a well-capitalized buyer could acquire using your business as a base?
> - [ ] - Is your industry consolidating? Are you a target, a consolidator, or both?
>
> - [ ] ### Strategic Value
>
> - [ ] - Are there larger companies in your industry or adjacent industries that would benefit from owning your business — for reasons beyond the financial returns?
> - [ ] - Have you been approached by potential buyers in the past? What came of it, and why didn't it proceed?
>
> - [ ] ---
>
> - [ ] ## Section 5: Preparedness
>
> - [ ] *Lens: Is the Seller — and the business — ready to go through a transaction?*
>
> - [ ] ### Financial Documentation
>
> - [ ] - Do you have three years of CPA-prepared P&L statements and tax returns available?
> - [ ] - Is your addback schedule documented with supporting evidence (receipts, payroll records, bank statements)?
> - [ ] - Is there anything about your financial records that you'd want to get ahead of before a buyer's accountants review them?
>
> - [ ] ### Business Documentation
>
> - [ ] - Do you have written contracts with your major customers?
> - [ ] - Do you have written employment agreements with key employees?
> - [ ] - Are your corporate records current — articles of incorporation, shareholder agreements, board minutes?
> - [ ] - Are there any pending or threatened lawsuits, regulatory investigations, or environmental issues I should know about?
>
> - [ ] ### Expectations and Timeline
>
> - [ ] - What do you think this business is worth? Where does that number come from?
> - [ ] - Have you had any informal conversations with buyers or advisors about value?
> - [ ] - What is your ideal timeline for closing a transaction?
> - [ ] - Are you flexible on deal structure — earnouts, seller notes, equity rollover — or are you looking for an all-cash close?
> - [ ] - What are your non-negotiables? What would cause you to walk away from an otherwise acceptable deal?
>
> - [ ] ### Professional Advisors
>
> - [ ] - Do you have a CPA who has experience with business transactions?
> - [ ] - Do you have an attorney you trust for business matters? Have they been involved in M&A transactions before?
> - [ ] - Are your financial and legal advisors aware you're exploring a sale?
>
> - [ ] ---
>
> - [ ] ## Closing (10 minutes)
>
> - [ ] *Summarize what you heard. Test the valuation expectations directly but respectfully.*
>
> - [ ] Suggested close:
>
> - [ ] *"Thank you — that's really helpful. Let me share a preliminary reaction, with the caveat that I haven't done a full analysis yet. Based on what I've heard, and what I know about your sector, businesses like yours are generally trading in the range of [X to Y times EBITDA], which would suggest a value range of approximately [A to B]. How does that land for you?"*
>
> - [ ] *Observe the response carefully. A Seller who engages constructively with the range — even if it's lower than they hoped — is demonstrating Preparedness and advisor receptivity. A Seller who dismisses the range without engaging with the reasoning is demonstrating a risk factor.*
>
> - [ ] Additional closing questions:
> - [ ] - Is there anything about your business or your situation that you haven't shared that I should know?
> - [ ] - What questions do you have for me about how Meritage works?
> - [ ] - What would make you confident that Meritage is the right advisor for this engagement?
>
> - [ ] ---
>
> - [ ] ## Post-Meeting Notes (complete within 24 hours)
>
> - [ ] Use this section to record observations that are not captured by the structured questions:
>
> - [ ] **Overall impression of the owner/Seller:**
>
> - [ ] **Most significant strengths observed:**
>
> - [ ] **Most significant concerns observed:**
>
> - [ ] **Information requested but not yet received:**
>
> - [ ] **Preliminary 5P ratings (before formal assessment):**
> - [ ] - People: Strong / Adequate / Weak / Disqualifying
> - [ ] - Performance: Strong / Adequate / Weak / Disqualifying
> - [ ] - Position: Strong / Adequate / Weak / Disqualifying
> - [ ] - Potential: Strong / Adequate / Weak / Disqualifying
> - [ ] - Preparedness: Strong / Adequate / Weak / Disqualifying
>
> - [ ] **Preliminary valuation expectation vs. market range:**
> - [ ] - Owner expectation: $___
> - [ ] - Preliminary market range: $___
> - [ ] - Gap: ___% above / below market
>
> - [ ] **Recommended next step:**
> - [ ] - [ ] Proceed to Qualification Score and formal assessment
> - [ ] - [ ] Request additional information before assessment
> - [ ] - [ ] Schedule follow-up conversation to cover gaps
> - [ ] - [ ] Decline — rationale: ___
>
